topic.what: Updated review of the Correa cascade — the stepwise progression from normal gastric mucosa through chronic gastritis, atrophic gastritis, intestinal metaplasia, dysplasia, to gastric adenocarcinoma — incorporating 20 years of molecular evidence since the original 1988 model.
topic.how: Expert synthesis of molecular, genetic, and epigenetic findings that refine each step of the cascade, including intestinal metaplasia subtypes and their differential cancer risk.
topic.why_matters: The Correa cascade remains the dominant conceptual framework for understanding gastric carcinogenesis and guides surveillance, risk stratification, and intervention timing decisions worldwide.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Degree to which the updated model integrates molecular mechanisms with the original histopathological progression
characteristics.value: comprehensive molecular update to each cascade stage
characteristics.vs_baseline: Substantially extends the 1988 Correa model with molecular markers, epigenetic alterations, and IM subtype distinctions
characteristics.evidence: Narrative synthesis of two decades of published molecular studies
characteristics.context: Intestinal-type gastric adenocarcinoma pathway; does not address diffuse-type carcinogenesis

good_for: Understanding the stepwise pathogenesis of intestinal-type gastric cancer
Identifying molecular markers for each precancerous stage
Guiding surveillance interval decisions based on cascade stage
not_for: Diffuse-type gastric cancer which follows a distinct molecular pathway
Predicting individual patient outcomes without additional clinical data
requires: Understanding of intestinal metaplasia subtypes (complete vs. incomplete)

novelty: Integrates molecular and epigenetic evidence into each stage of the original 1988 Correa cascade
Refines the concept of a 'point of no return' in the precancerous progression with molecular markers
open_problems: Precise molecular definition of the point of no return beyond which eradication no longer prevents cancer
Prognostic value of incomplete versus complete intestinal metaplasia subtypes
Integration of host genetic susceptibility factors into the cascade model
